seo.title: Building Voice Agents with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: BeeAI, IBM's open-source framework, enables building production-grade voice agents with memory, tools, and observability for real-time conversational AI. Voice agents demand sub-second latency and perfect reliability, making guardrails essential to prevent unbounded memory growth, tool injection attacks, and incoherent conversations. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response safety, latency compliance, and conversation coherence in high-risk voice pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents with BeeAI follow a real-time pipeline: microphone audio streams through speech-to-text (STT) for transcription, feeding into BeeAI's agent core for LLM reasoning with memory and tools. The agent maintains conversation state across turns, invoking tools like database queries or APIs when needed. The LLM response is routed to text-to-speech (TTS) for audio playback, all within sub-second end-to-end latency.

BeeAI's event-driven architecture emits hooks for agent actions (tool calls, memory updates, final responses), ideal for Vex integration. Memory persistence handles long sessions, but risks unbounded growth without bounds checking. The pipeline requires safety filtering at each stage to block harmful content.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Mic → [STT: Whisper] → BeeAI Agent Core → [LLM: Granite] → [Tools/DB] → [TTS: Piper] → Speaker
                 ↑                           ↓
              Vex Listener ← Events: memory/tool/response

Agent Memory ↔ Persistence Layer (Redis)
Latency Budget: STT<200ms + LLM<400ms + TTS<200ms

risks.name: Unbounded Agent Memory Growth
risks.description: Long voice sessions accumulate conversation history without eviction, causing OOM errors and slow reasoning
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory token count per session and enforces eviction policies

risks.name: Tool Injection via STT
risks.description: Adversarial audio inputs craft tool calls bypassing validation, executing unauthorized actions
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool parameters against schemas before execution

risks.name: Latency Budget Violations
risks.description: Pipeline stages exceed sub-second requirements, causing unnatural conversation flow
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces per-stage and end-to-end latency SLOs with circuit breaking

risks.name: Incoherent Conversation State
risks.description: Serialization errors corrupt memory state, causing hallucinated context or repetitive responses
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies memory state integrity and response coherence against conversation history

implementation.title: Basic BeeAI Voice Agent Pipeline
implementation.description: Core pipeline integrating STT, BeeAI agent, and TTS with event hooks for Vex
implementation.code:
import beeai
from vex import VexClient
import whisper, piper_tts

# Initialize components
stt = whisper.load_model('tiny.en')
agent = beeai.Agent(model='granite-3b')
agent.add_tool(beeai.Tool('query_db', schema={'sql': 'string'}))

# Vex client for observability
vex = VexClient(api_key='vex-key')

class VoiceAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.memory = {}
        
    async def process_audio(self, audio_bytes):
        # STT
        text = stt.transcribe(audio_bytes)['text']
        
        # Agent reasoning with events
        with agent.events() as events:
            response = await agent.run(text, memory=self.memory)
            
            # Emit to Vex
            for event in events:
                vex.record_event('beeai_event', {'type': event.type, 'data': event.data})
        
        # TTS
        audio_out = piper_tts.synthesize(response)
        return audio_out

implementation.description: Implement conversation memory with automatic truncation to prevent unbounded growth

implementation.description: Strict tool parameter validation prevents injection attacks from STT input

implementation.title: Latency Monitoring Wrapper
implementation.description: Wrap pipeline stages with latency guards for sub-second performance
implementation.code:
import time
from vex import VexClient

class LatencyGuard:
    def __init__(self):
        self.budgets = {'stt': 0.2, 'llm': 0.4, 'tts': 0.2}
    
    async def measure(self, stage, func, *args):
        start = time.time()
        result = await func(*args)
        latency = time.time() - start
        
        if latency > self.budgets[stage]:
            vex.record_violation('latency_budget', {'stage': stage, 'measured': latency})
            raise TimeoutError(f'{stage} exceeded budget')
        
        vex.record_metric('latency', {'stage': stage, 'value': latency})
        return result

# Usage
guard = LatencyGuard()
text = await guard.measure('stt', stt.transcribe, audio_bytes)
response = await guard.measure('llm', agent.run, text)
audio = await guard.measure('tts', piper_tts.synthesize, response)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Event Listener
vexIntegration.description: Capture all BeeAI events for comprehensive observability and verification

vexIntegration.title: Response Safety Filtering
vexIntegration.description: Real-time content moderation for voice responses with safety verdicts
vexIntegration.code:
async def safe_voice_response(agent_output: str) -> str:
    # Vex safety check
    safety_verdict = vex.verify('response_appropriateness', {
        'text': agent_output,
        'categories': ['hate', 'violence', 'sexual'],
        'threshold': 0.95
    })
    
    if not safety_verdict:
        # Fallback safe response
        return "I can't assist with that request. Let's talk about something else."
    
    return agent_output

# In main pipeline
response = await agent.run(user_input)
safe_response = await safe_voice_response(response)
audio = piper_tts.synthesize(safe_response)

# Log verdict
vex.record_verdict('safety_filter', {'passed': safety_verdict, 'input': response[:100]})

vexIntegration.title: Conversation Coherence Guard
vexIntegration.description: Verify response maintains context from conversation history
